description: Fix two CLI UX issues found during fresh-user testing: (1) `opentabs config set browser-tool.` (with trailing dot, no value) should list available browser tools, matching the existing `tool.` behavior; (2) README.md quick start section sends users through an unnecessary manual secret extraction step when `opentabs start` already outputs ready-to-copy MCP client config with the secret pre-filled. LOW severity issues not addressed: `config set browser-tool.<name> disabled` could confirm the effect (e.g., 'This tool will no longer be available to MCP clients'), and `opentabs stop` hint about Ctrl+C is slightly misleading for users who started the server via nohup/docker exec -d.

userStories.title: Add browser-tool listing to `config set browser-tool.`
userStories.description: As a user, I want `opentabs config set browser-tool.` (with trailing dot, no value) to list all available browser tools, so that I can discover browser tool names for enabling/disabling — matching the existing `opentabs config set tool.` behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: `opentabs config set browser-tool.` (with trailing dot, no value) prints a list of all browser tool names fetched from the running server
The output format mirrors `handleListTools`: a bold 'Available browser tools:' header, indented tool names, and a usage hint line
When the server is not reachable, prints a helpful error message directing the user to start the server
The existing `opentabs config set tool.` behavior is unchanged
`opentabs config set browser-tool.<name> enabled|disabled` continues to work as before
userStories.notes:
File: `platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts`. The fix is in `handleConfigSet()` (line ~543). Currently line 548 checks `key === TOOL_PREFIX` ('tool.') and calls `handleListTools()`. But there's no equivalent check for `key === BROWSER_TOOL_PREFIX` ('browser-tool.'). When user types `browser-tool.` with no value, it falls through to line 552 (`!value` check) which prints 'Missing value' — wrong behavior.

Fix: Add a new check `if (key === BROWSER_TOOL_PREFIX) return handleListBrowserTools(options);` BEFORE the `!value` check at line 552. Create a new `handleListBrowserTools()` function modeled on `handleListTools()` (line 265-290) that calls the existing `fetchBrowserToolNames()` (line 247-263) and prints the results. Use the same output format: bold header, indented names, usage hint.

userStories.title: Update README quick start to reference `opentabs start` output instead of manual secret extraction
userStories.description: As a new user reading the README, I want the MCP client configuration instructions to direct me to copy the ready-to-copy config from the `opentabs start` output, so that I don't waste time running a separate jq command and manually replacing a placeholder.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Configure your MCP client' section in README.md no longer tells users to run `opentabs config show --json --show-secret | jq -r .secret`
Instead, it tells users that `opentabs start` prints ready-to-copy MCP client config with the secret pre-filled on first run
It mentions `opentabs config show --show-secret` as a way to see the config again later (not as the primary path)
The JSON example block with YOUR_SECRET_HERE is removed or replaced with a note that the actual config is shown in the `opentabs start` output
userStories.notes:
File: `README.md` (repo root), lines 59-81. Currently tells users to run `opentabs config show --json --show-secret | jq -r .secret` and manually replace YOUR_SECRET_HERE in a JSON block. But `opentabs start` already shows the full config with the actual secret pre-filled on first run. The README should direct users to simply copy the config block from the `opentabs start` output.

Rewrite the section to:
1. State that `opentabs start` shows ready-to-copy MCP client config on first run (for Claude Code and other clients)
2. Tell users to copy the config block from the terminal output into their MCP client config file
3. Mention `opentabs config show --show-secret` and `opentabs start --show-config` as ways to see the config blocks again later
4. Remove the `jq` command and the JSON block with YOUR_SECRET_HERE placeholder
